experiences.description: FinTech - Buy Now, Pay Later solution for merchants and private users - Migration of processes  from monolith to event-driven architecture - Design and implement system like auto/manual reconciliation, invoices notifications, scheduling task to check for due invoices, dispute invoices, direct - Implement  multiple features using Ruby on Rails, Typescript and ReactJS - Migrate RSpec Tests to a load testing tool (K6) in Javascript and integrate them into our development pipelines - Participate in discussions with product and tech team about new features - Mentoring/collaborate new developers and frontend developers to understand and get involved in  our internal systems

Digital leasing platform - Built, deployed and migrated  from monolith to microservices on AWS (EC2, - Lead Automatization of Zoho CRM processes to reduce manual work and improve sales team efficiency. - Leading automatization of Zoho CRM to  improve sales team efficiency. - Developed features in Ruby on Rails, typescript, vuejs, react,  including fleet management, admin, and car dealer dashboards, lease car configurations, internationalization,  A/B testing, SEO features, and data scraping. - Collaborated with the product team on requirement estimation and refinement - Monitored and resolved bugs using Datadog and New Relic. - Mentored junior developers. - Use agile methodologies like Kanban to Scrum.

In my final year of university, I began working as a freelance developer, where I gained experience using English in a professional environment. I contributed to maintaining and developing features for a Ruby on Rails monolithic application, working across the full stack—from user interface design to troubleshooting and problem-solving

In university times, my friends and I had a lot fun developing software. We worked on personal projects and even got a few contracts from clients. Two projects that we managed to release to specific clients were: - Susy: A tool for IT support and maintenance used by a school in Barranquilla. - Frepi: An eCommerce project aimed at making it easier for farmers to sell directly to consumers.
Led classes, created assessments, and offered guidance to help students enhance their programming skills in courses such as: Data Structures & Algorithms and Basic Programming
